reason: Both correctly conclude "fix it, don't kill it" and cite the ~2.2x LTV:CAC and ~6-month payback. A covers more of the specifically flagged fixes: branded-vs-generic split (which also surfaces the blended-vs-paid-CAC nuance), landing page/offer conversion improvement with a concrete number, negative keywords, and a useful sanity check on the LTV/retention assumption. B is well-organized and adds one good point not in the list (optimize tracking to closed-won revenue) plus a nod to SEO/partnerships as alternative channels, but it omits the branded/generic split and landing page fix entirely despite promising prioritized fixes — two of the rubric's named levers. On balance A covers more of the decision-relevant specifics without added length or padding.
